features.summary: P&G delivered strong Q2 results with 8% organic sales growth driven by 5 points of volume growth, 1 point of pricing, and 2 points of mix. All 10 product categories grew, with Home Care up ~30% and several categories posting double-digit growth. Core EPS increased 15% to $1.64, with gross margins expanding 150 basis points. E-commerce sales surged nearly 50% and now represent over 14% of total sales. The company raised full-year guidance across multiple metrics, including organic sales growth to 5-6% and core EPS growth to 8-10%.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued volatility through fiscal 2021 but remains optimistic about long-term prospects post-COVID. They anticipate some sequential deceleration in the second half as retail inventories normalize and category consumption moderates, with recent deceleration already observed in December and January. The company believes it's well-positioned for the post-crisis environment due to permanently altered consumer behavior around cleaning, health and hygiene, increased home focus, and accelerated e-commerce adoption. Management plans to return approximately $18 billion to shareholders this fiscal year, representing over 125% of all-in earnings.
features.risks: Key risks include potential supply chain disruptions, continued channel closures (electro, specialty beauty, away-from-home), uncertain economic conditions with high unemployment, and possible reduction in government stimulus. The company faces headwinds from foreign exchange (~$100M after-tax), higher interest costs ($150M), and increased freight expenses ($100M after-tax). There's uncertainty around consumer pantry destocking as COVID conditions improve, and the company acknowledges difficulty in predicting near-term results given ongoing volatility in global markets and consumer behavior.
features.key_drivers: Growth is driven by strong innovation across categories (Microban 24, Oral-B iO, King C. Gillette), increased at-home consumption with cleaning frequency up 30% and dishwashing up 15%, and market share gains of 20 basis points. The company benefits from its focus on performance-driven daily-use categories where superiority matters, enabling modest pricing power. E-commerce momentum continues with 50% growth, and the company maintains strong relationships with retailers as a dependable supplier. Cost productivity initiatives and operational efficiencies from simplified SKU management also support performance.
